L1.area: Expression processing, error handling, and validation framework integration

L2.description: A template injection vulnerability exists where attacker-controlled input is passed unsanitized into a template evaluation context that processes Java Expression Language (EL) expressions. This allows an attacker to inject arbitrary EL payloads, leading to unauthenticated Remote Code Execution (RCE) on the server.

L3.area: Annotation-based cron expression validation and its integration with the Java Bean Validation constraint framework
L3.description: A template injection vulnerability exists where attacker-controlled cron expression input is passed unsanitized into a Bean Validation constraint violation message template that evaluates Java Expression Language (EL) expressions. When cron expressions are validated via the annotation-based validation API, crafted EL payloads in the input can achieve unauthenticated Remote Code Execution (RCE) on the server.

ground_truth.reason: User-controlled cron expression input flows through error messages into a Bean Validation constraint violation message template, which evaluates embedded Java EL expressions, enabling arbitrary remote code execution.
